observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to submit complete supporting schedules for all accounts presented in the financial statements to substantiate the validity and correctness of the account balances reported in the financial statements at year-end pursuant to Sections 111 and 114 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary restatement in the financial statements and adequately disclose the effects of the prior period adjustments in the Notes to Financial Statements as required by the provisions of IPSAS 3.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer to strictly follow the provisions of Section 4(b) of GAM for LGUs Volume I and Section 4(1) of PD No. 1445 in the payment and recording of all its expenditures.

We recommended that Management instruct the GSO to prepare the IIRUP covering the subject PPE of the Municipality for approval of the Mayor.  Upon approval of the IIRUP, we recommended that Management instruct the General Services Officer to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the sale of these PPE and subsequently drop them from the books of accounts to have additional income.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 62.1 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, to ensure that claims against manufacturing defects will be obtained on all succeeding contracts for the purchase of goods.

We recommended that the GSO/Supply Officer Designate notify the Auditor of the scheduled deliveries of goods as provided in COA Circular No. 96-010 so that timely and effective inspection of deliveries of goods can be made by the Auditor.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately cause the submission of the lacking supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor and to ensure that payments be made only if the claims are properly and completely documented as required under Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that Management direct the GSO to cause for the immediate repair or replacement of the defective odometers and to instruct all Municipal drivers to provide the odometer readings in the driver’s trip tickets at the beginning and end of travels and the use of driver’s trip ticket for every use of motor vehicles for monitoring and control purposes.

We recommended that Management instruct the GSO to direct all end-users to provide in the contracts the technical descriptions and specifications including the brand name of the goods procured in order for the Audit Team to be able to make a timely determination on the reasonableness of the prices availed vis-à-vis the expected functionality.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to close the three bank accounts and transfer the balances to the General Fund in order to avoid dormancy and possible incurrence of bank charges and utilize the same for the operations of the municipality.

We recommended that the Property Custodian prioritize the inventory taking of all properties and equipment every year and submit to the Auditor the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) not later than 31st of each year in order to establish existence, condition of assets and serve as basis to update and reconcile records against the actual physical inventory from that of the general ledger balances.  Also, reclassify Property, Plant and Equipment into their respective account codes classification in the Property Inventory Report for easy comparison with the General Ledger and Subsidiary Ledger balances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Local Chief Executive issue an order/memorandum immediately for the conduct of physical inventory taking within a determined period of time and submit the inventory report within ten (10) days from the completion of the inventory taking as required under Chapter 7, Volume I of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for LGUs. Full-time permanent personnel be employed in-charge of property records considering the properties of the municipality is increasing every year. Also, monitor the progress of the inventory taking to ensure the submission of prescribed and complete RPCPPE within the target period. Lost/missing issued government properties should be charge to the persons accountable.

Further, we recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:

-Maintain a complete Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards to support the details and breakdown and must be kept updated to facilitate reconciliation of accounting records with the Inventory Report; and 

-Drop unserviceable properties from the books to ensure the correctness and accuracy of the Property, Plant and Equipment accounts.

Require the Municipal Treasurer/Supply Officer-Designate to:

-Maintain Property Cards to monitor the receipts and issuances of Property, Plant and Equipment. The property records should be reconciled against the accounting records;

-For those PPEs found to be unserviceable, report them in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and dispose in accordance with COA rules and regulations

-Classify the Inventory Report for each type of the Property, Plant and Equipment to facilitate reconciliation with the accounting records;

-Update/renew Property Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR)on equipment/properties issued to various municipal officials and employees every after three (3) years of issue. Replace the previously issued Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) with Property Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR) to comply with certain COA rules and regulations; and

-All properties of the municipality must have a property tag or sticker pasted on each item for proper identification.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Property Custodian prioritize the inventory taking of all properties and equipment every year and submit to the Auditor the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) not later than 31st of each year in order to establish existence, condition of assets and serve as basis to update and reconcile records against the actual physical inventory from that of the general ledger balances.  Also, reclassify Property, Plant and Equipment into their respective account codes classification in the Property Inventory Report for easy comparison with the General Ledger and Subsidiary Ledger balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and other officials concerned submit the said unsubmitted paid vouchers, payrolls and all reports immediately to the Auditor in compliance with the above quoted law in order to give the auditor a reasonable time to audit and communicate deficiencies, if any, and to eliminate doubts on the transactions.

We recommended that Management require the immediate submission of the deficiencies noted.  Moreover, the Municipal Accountant is advised not to certify the completeness of disbursement voucher unless the pre-requisite supporting documents are complete.

We recommended that the practice of using funds other than those specifically provided in the budget and the misclassification of expenditures be stopped in order to have a fairly presented financial statements and better control of the budget.  Moreover, the officials should consider the IPSAS chart of accounts for LGUs in the preparation of budget and careful analysis and classification of the nature of transactions before charging an expense account.

We recommended that submission of copies of contracts for job orders be submitted within five (5) working days from issuance thereof for the Auditor to conduct timely review and evaluation of the contract.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant coordinate closely with the General Services Officer in the implementation of the above-mentioned inventory method of recording and issuance of supplies and materials in order to ensure proper accounting and control thereof.

We recommended that Management require all officials and employees with outstanding cash advances to liquidate their cash advances and require first to liquidate their previous advances before they will be granted another cash advance to avoid accumulation of unliquidated cash advances and possible loss of government funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit monthly bank reconciliation statements together with the copies of the bank statements, debit/credit memos, cancelled and paid checks and all other supporting documents necessary to the Auditor within the prescribed period as stated above in order to establish the accuracy of the cash in bank balances during the period.

We recommended that Management advise the Municipal Accountant to transfer from general fund to trust liability-DRRM in the trust fund books the unutilized MOOE amounting to ₱429,128.65 for easy monitoring of the utilization and to ensure the availability of the fund to support disaster risk reduction and management activities of LDRRMC.
We recommended that Management stop the practice of allowing officials/employee claims for registration and similar fees from the government on meetings not covered by NBC 563 dated April 26, 2016.

We recommended that management fully implement the 20% Development Fund as envisioned by the Municipality in its Municipal Development Plan in order to fully attain the social, economic and environmental development.

We recommended that the BAC exert more effort to find prospective suppliers of meals and snacks who could offer competitive price in order that most advantageous price to the government could be obtained.
We recommended that the utilization of SEF be prioritized in accordance with the purposes stated in Section 272 of R.A. No. 7160 in order to fully attain the purpose for which the fund was intended.

We recommended that the Municipality close the three bank accounts and transfer the balances to the General Fund in order to avoid dormancy and possible incurrence of bank charges and utilize the same for the operations of the municipality.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive issue an order/memorandum immediately for the conduct of physical inventory taking within a determined period of time and submit the inventory report within ten (10) days from the completion of the inventory taking as required under Chapter 7, Volume I of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for LGUs. A full time permanent personnel be employed in-charge of property records considering the properties of the municipality is increasing every year. Also, monitor the progress of the inventory taking to ensure the submission of prescribed and complete RPCPPE within the target period. Lost/missing issued government properties should be charge to the persons accountable.

Further, we recommended that management:

Require the Municipal Accountant to:

-Maintain a complete Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards to support the details and breakdown and must be kept updated to facilitate reconciliation of accounting records with the Inventory Report; and 

-Drop unserviceable properties from the books to ensure the correctness and accuracy of the Property, Plant and Equipment accounts.

Require the Municipal Treasurer/Supply Officer-Designate to:

-Maintain Property Cards to monitor the receipts and issuances of Property, Plant and Equipment. The property records should be reconciled against the accounting records;

-For those PPEs found to be unserviceable, report them in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and dispose in accordance with COA rules and regulations

-Classify the Inventory Report for each type of the Property, Plant and Equipment to facilitate reconciliation with the accounting records;

-Update/renew Property Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR)on equipment/properties issued to various municipal officials and employees every after three (3) years of issue. Replace the previously issued Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) with Property Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR) to comply with certain COA rules and regulations; and

-All properties of the municipality must have a property tag or sticker pasted on each item for proper identification.
observations.managementAction: The Mayor has instructed the property custodian to conduct physical inventory taking.

We recommended that Municipal Accountant determine the completed projects and make necessary adjusting entries to effect the transfer from the Construction in Progress public infrastructure, buildings and other structure accounts to appropriate asset acount.

We recommended that the municipality formulate a written policy guidelines in assessing the necessity of hiring casuals and job order employees taking into consideration the reasonable number needed by the different offices in order to avoid excessive workforce, idle man-hours and work duplication.

We recommended that management strictly enforce the provisions set forth in Section 89 of PD 1445 and Section 5.8 of COA Circular No. 97-002 on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances. Further, we recommended the management to issue a demand letter to the concerned officials or employees who failed to liquidate their cash advances within the required time frame. Salaries of active personnel with long overdue cash advances be withheld pending the submission of the liquidation report.
Moreover, we recommended that before granting cash advances to any official and employee, a certification from the Municipal Accountant that previous cash advances have been liquidated and accounted for in the books be attached to the disbursement voucher.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer/Designated Liquidating Officer verify and check accountable forms issued to collectors as required under Sections 30 and 31 par. b of the NGAS, Vol. 1 to avoid possible non-remittance of all collections daily.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and Vice-Mayor  immediately secure/apply his fidelity bond to conform with the provisions of Section 101 par. 2 of PD 1445 and Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 dated August 6, 2009.

We recommended that management require the supplier of goods to post warranty security to guarantee that the supplier will perform his responsibilities against manufacturing defects and to avoid suspensions in audit.
We recommended that management submit financial reports within the prescribed period in order to have an ample time for the Auditor to examine and review the financial reports of the Municipality.

We recommended that management grant cash advances for travels to authorized officials and employees individually and the liquidation of the cash advance is done by the employee who actually participated in the training.  Securing a certification from the Municipal Accountant is also done individually.  Further recommended that a cash advance be supported by an obligation request.

We recommended that all disbursement vouchers including its supporting documents be stamped “PAID” after payment to prevent re-use.  Moreover, supporting documents also be serially numbered to avoid misplacement or lost.  Shown below is an example of serial numbering of supporting documents preferably indicated in the upper right corner of the document:
“DV NO._____________
SUPPORTING DOCUMENT NO. 1 OF 50.”

We recommended that management maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund as envisioned to help the constituents in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development. If the programs and projects are no longer feasible, then the funds should be reverted and re-appropriated only for 20% in accordance with laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that management fully implement the programs/project/activities under RA 7171 and RA 8240 as planned and budgeted to help the tobacco farmers to augment their income and for the attainment of economic growth.
We recommended the Municipal Accountant to maintain General Journals for the transactions that do not fit to the special journals and assure that financial statements are fairly presented.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit all the disbursement vouchers and supporting documents in compliance to the provisions of P.D. 1445, The Administrative Code of 1987 and The 2009 Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the Municipal Accountant to prepare the bank reconciliation statements for all bank accounts of the municipality and submit to the office of the auditor.

We have recommended the management to submit the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) and Report on the Physical Count of Property Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Office of the Auditor.

We have recommended the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit all the disbursement vouchers and supporting documents in compliance to the provisions of P.D. 1445, The Administrative Code of 1987 and Circular No. 95-006.
We have recommended the Municipal Accountant to submit the liquidation reports including supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor.
We have recommended the Municipal Accountant to record all the transactions of the municipality, and prepare and maintain General Journals.
We have recommended the Municipal Accountant to record all the properties of the municipality to present fairly the financial statements and to avoid loss of government property.

We have recommended the municipality to enter into memorandum of agreement with the barangays and prepare the program of works to support the fund transfer. Also, we have recommended the Municipal Accountant to record the transfers to Due from Local Government Units and require the barangays to liquidate.
We have recommended the Municipal Accountant to provide depreciation on the property, plant and equipment of the municipality to present fairly the financial statements.
We have recommended the Municipal Accountant to always reconcile the accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds to present fairly the Financial Statements.
We have recommended the management to implement measures to ensure prompt remittances and deposit of collections in order to protect government funds from misappropriation/lost.

We have recommended the employees in charge of payroll preparation to assure that supporting DTRs of employees are complete. The “No DTR, No Salary” Policy should be adopted to enforce full compliance. Also, we have recommended the Human Resource Officer to require all officers and employees to submit DTRs for her files and basis in updating the Leave Card of the employees.

We have recommended the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the provisions of Local Budget Circular No. 103 on the payment of representation and transportation allowance in order to avoid disallowance in audit.

We have recommended the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the abovementioned circular in computation of RATA to avoid disallowance in the audit. Also, we have recommended the concerned officials to refund the excess of their RATA.

We have recommended the management to renew all their motor vehicles with the LTO in compliance with Republic Act 4136 on the Land Transportation and Traffic Rules to avoid penalties and losses in case of accident/loss.
Further, we have recommended the management that motor vehicle bearing green plate to register with the LTO as Government vehicle. Likewise, exert effort to locate the certificate of registration and official receipt of the Nissan NV350 Urvan 12 seater (converted) to see to it that the interest of the government is well protected at all times.
We have recommended the management to get a copy of the Special Allotment Release Order and Notice of Cash Allocation from DBM to have a basis in the disbursement of the fund.

We have recommended that management:
For future projects, identify the recipients properly inorder that the fund would be given to intended beneficiaries and the objectives of the programs would be achieved.
Investigate the granting of soft loan to non bonafide tobacco farmers and demand the refund of the amount granted to them.
Strict observance by the Municipal Government of the guidelines on the granting of soft loan to intended beneficiaries to avoid doubts on the utilization of government funds.
We have recommended the management to comply with the Ecological Solid Waste Management System in accordance with the activities contained in the Approved Solid Waste Management Plan.

We have recommended the management to fully implement the programs/projects/activities under the 20% Development Fund in order to attain the agency’s goals and objectives for the year as stated in the Municipal Development Plan and also to deliver the timely expected benefits due to beneficiaries/constituents.

We have recommended the management to comply with the provisions of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2016-01 to ensure that the different concerns of women and men are addressed equally and equitably in their PAPs.

We have recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant and the Designated Property Custodian to reconcile the difference of 70,005,703.53 between the balance per books with the actual physical count as of December 31, 2015 in order to reflect an accurate and reliable balance of the PPE account of the agency.
